components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: base16ct
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of Base16 a.k.a hexadecimal (RFC 4648) which avoids any usages of data-dependent branches/LUTs and thereby provides portable "best effort" constant-time operation and embedded-friendly no_std support

components.name: base64

components.author: Alex Crichton <alex@alexcrichton.com>
components.name: cfg-if
components.description: A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg] parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the item that gets emitted.

components.description: A simple to use, efficient, and full-featured Command Line Argument Parser

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: const-oid
components.description: Const-friendly implementation of the ISO/IEC Object Identifier (OID) standard as defined in ITU X.660, with support for BER/DER encoding/decoding as well as heapless no_std (i.e. embedded) support

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: cpufeatures
components.description: Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets,  with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: crypto-bigint
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of a big integer library which has been designed from the ground-up for use in cryptographic applications. Provides constant-time, no_std-friendly implementations of modern formulas using const generics.

components.name: crypto-common

components.name: der
components.description: Pure Rust embedded-friendly implementation of the Distinguished Encoding Rules (DER) for Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) as described in ITU X.690 with full support for heapless no_std targets

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: digest

components.name: ecdsa

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: elliptic-curve
components.description: General purpose Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) support, including types and traits for representing various elliptic curve forms, scalars, points, and public/secret keys composed thereof.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: p256
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of the NIST P-256 (a.k.a. secp256r1, prime256v1) elliptic curve as defined in SP 800-186, with support for ECDH, ECDSA signing/verification, and general purpose curve arithmetic

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: pem-rfc7468
components.description: PEM Encoding (RFC 7468) for PKIX, PKCS, and CMS Structures, implementing a strict subset of the original Privacy-Enhanced Mail encoding intended specifically for use with cryptographic keys, certificates, and other messages. Provides a no_std-friendly, constant-time implementation suitable for use with cryptographic private keys.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: pkcs8
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #8: Private-Key Information Syntax Specification (RFC 5208), with additional support for PKCS#8v2 asymmetric key packages (RFC 5958)

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: primeorder
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of complete addition formulas for prime order elliptic curves (Renes-Costello-Batina 2015). Generic over field elements and curve equation coefficients

components.author: David Tolnay <dtolnay@gmail.com>, Alex Crichton <alex@alexcrichton.com>
components.name: proc-macro2

components.name: secrecy
components.description: Wrapper types and traits for secret management which help ensure they aren't accidentally copied, logged, or otherwise exposed (as much as possible), and also ensure secrets are securely wiped from memory when dropped.

components.description: Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at     compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed     integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its     implementation is incomplete.

components.author: The RustCrypto Project Developers
